acceptance_targets: [ch02_f01] A single, consistent marriage duration appears throughout the chapter. The number matches the arithmetic of the stated wedding date and the story year (2029) to within a reasonable rounding margin of no more than 6 months. No two references to the marriage's length differ by more than one year.

[ch02_f02] Every reference to Mira's proxy tenure in the chapter matches the continuity sheet's canonical figure. The same duration appears in all three locations (lines 3, 15, 23) without internal contradiction.

[ch02_f06] The stretch from line 25 to line 45 contains at least 2 scene-level moments — physical actions, sensory details, or interpersonal micro-events — that interrupt the analytical register and anchor the interiority in the body. The language metaphor at line 43 is no longer than 3 sentences.

[ch02_f07] The notification discovery section (lines 91-127) contains at least 2 physical/visceral responses beyond 'hands gone still' — one at the point of first comprehension (around line 109) and one at the recognition of systemic opacity (around line 119). At least one metaphor in this section has a violent or physical edge consistent with Mira's stress profile in the style bible.

findings.acceptance_test: A single, consistent marriage duration appears throughout the chapter. The number matches the arithmetic of the stated wedding date and the story year (2029) to within a reasonable rounding margin of no more than 6 months. No two references to the marriage's length differ by more than one year.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:11 says 'Fourteen years of marriage had made them experts at the precise weight of what went unsaid.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65 gives the wedding date as 'June 14, 2017.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:85 says 'Twelve years of marriage compressed into twenty minutes.' The case number NYC-FAM-2029-041187 (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:83) places the story in 2029. June 2017 to March 2029 is approximately 11 years and 9 months.
findings.problem: The chapter gives two contradictory marriage durations — 'fourteen years' on line 11 and 'twelve years' on line 85 — and neither matches the arithmetic of the stated wedding date (June 14, 2017) against the story year (2029, per the case number). This is an internal self-contradiction and a timeline violation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile all three data points at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:85. If the wedding date stays June 14, 2017, and the story is set in early March 2029, the marriage is approximately 11 years and 9 months. Change line 11 from 'fourteen years' to 'nearly twelve years' or 'twelve years.' Line 85's 'twelve years' can stand as reasonable rounding. Alternatively, if fourteen years is the intended duration, adjust the wedding date to approximately June 2015.

findings.acceptance_test: Every reference to Mira's proxy tenure in the chapter matches the continuity sheet's canonical figure. The same duration appears in all three locations (lines 3, 15, 23) without internal contradiction.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:3 says Mira calculated her subway mark 'her second week on the commute. Fourteen months ago.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23 says 'Fourteen months of the same coffee.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:15 says 'She had looked at this photo every morning for fourteen months.' The continuity sheet states: 'The Kesslers have been in proxy roles for about eighteen months.'
findings.problem: The chapter consistently places Mira's proxy tenure at approximately 14 months. The continuity sheet gives approximately 18 months. This is a 3-4 month discrepancy between the chapter and the canonical continuity record.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either update snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:15, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23 — changing 'fourteen months' to 'eighteen months' or 'a year and a half' — to match the continuity sheet, or coordinate a continuity-sheet update if 14 months is the intended canonical duration. All three instances must be updated together.

findings.acceptance_test: The stretch from line 25 to line 45 contains at least 2 scene-level moments — physical actions, sensory details, or interpersonal micro-events — that interrupt the analytical register and anchor the interiority in the body. The language metaphor at line 43 is no longer than 3 sentences.
findings.evidence: The stretch from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:25 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45 is approximately 20 lines of sustained analytical interiority about Mira's competence loss, including the extended 'foreign language' metaphor (line 43), Dan's remembered comment and Mira's analysis of its imprecision (line 45), and multiple paragraphs of essayistic reflection. No scene-level event, physical action, or sensory interruption occurs in this stretch.
findings.problem: The passage reads as a character-study essay rather than a scene. While individual observations are sharp ('Her strategic mind, her pattern recognition... Gemini-state had no use for any of it'), the density of unbroken analysis without embodied interruption makes the prose static. The style bible warns against 'prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding.' The language metaphor at line 43 is abstract and extended beyond what its insight warrants — 4+ sentences elaborating a single conceit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the analytical run between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:25 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45 by anchoring at least 2 observations in specific physical moments — a sensory detail from the briefing review (the feel of the document, the screen's brightness, a sound from the office), a body sensation during or after the presentation, or a micro-interaction with another proxy. Compress the language metaphor at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:43 from 4+ sentences to 2, letting the image arrive without exhaustive elaboration.

findings.acceptance_test: The notification discovery section (lines 91-127) contains at least 2 physical/visceral responses beyond 'hands gone still' — one at the point of first comprehension (around line 109) and one at the recognition of systemic opacity (around line 119). At least one metaphor in this section has a violent or physical edge consistent with Mira's stress profile in the style bible.
findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127, Mira discovers the infrastructure flag on her divorce. Her response is rendered almost entirely through analytical processing: reading the notification three times (line 109), searching the protocol document (line 111), recognizing the information architecture (line 113), filling out the contact form (lines 121-123), noting the recursive timeline (line 127). Physical/visceral alarm is limited to 'her hands gone still on the keyboard' and 'the apartment's ambient noise sharpening around her' (line 119).
findings.problem: The chapter's texture mode is 'controlled then alarming.' The proxy workday is beautifully controlled; the notification discovery should shift into alarm. But Mira's response stays in her strategic-analyst register throughout — she processes the shock as information rather than experiencing it as threat. The style bible specifies Mira's stress tells include violent metaphors and clipped syntax. Neither appears in this stretch. The 'alarming' half of the texture mode reads as intellectual puzzle rather than visceral disturbance. The body is underrepresented at the chapter's emotional climax.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the stretch from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109 to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127, introduce 2-3 moments where Mira's analytical composure cracks: a physical sensation (stomach dropping, temperature change, tightness in chest or throat), a fragmented thought that doesn't complete, or a metaphor that turns violent or physical per her stress profile. The analytical processing can remain dominant — it IS her character — but the body needs to interrupt the mind at least twice. Target especially snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109 (the initial re-reading, where comprehension should register physically) and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:119 (the recognition of what she can't position against, where the style bible's 'clipped syntax' stress tell should surface).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's risk zone is the gap between dignity and absurdity. The characters are adults with professional histories, a child, a mortgage — people with legitimate self-respect — who have been reduced to warm bodies in an optimization framework they cannot understand. The comedy must not protect them from the humiliation; the humiliation must not neutralize the comedy. Render both registers at full force, often in the same scene. The temptation will be to soften the comedy into gentle satire or to darken the humiliation into dystopian grimness — resist both. The novel's territory is the painful and the funny coexisting without one defusing the other. Commit to the specific physical embarrassment of being professionally irrelevant. Commit to the specific tenderness of parenting under surveillance. Commit to the specific grief of a marriage that might still be alive inside a system that has classified it as infrastructure. When in doubt, choose the version that makes you wince and then laugh, or laugh and then wince — both are correct.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene requires the reader to sit with discomfort — economic shame, marital cruelty, parental failure, institutional humiliation, the specific indignity of being professionally redundant — render it with full specificity. 'They struggled with the situation' is not a sentence that belongs in this novel. Name the thing. Show the body. Let the reader be uncomfortable.
aesthetic_risk_policy.intimacy_and_desire_policy: Dan and Mira's residual physical attraction is a narrative force, not background radiation. When the novel's relational pressure calls for it, render desire with the same specificity as any other emotional state — where in the body it lives, how it changes the air in a room, the precise quality of proximity when two people who know each other's bodies are trying to pretend they don't. The almost-kiss in Chapter 7 should be as fully rendered as any confrontation scene.
aesthetic_risk_policy.register_commitment: This is a dark comedy, not a cautionary tale, not a dystopia, not a satire with a thesis. The novel should not argue about AI. It should render what it feels like to be a body in a world where bodies are overhead. The comic register is not a veneer over a serious message — it IS the message. A scene that is genuinely funny about genuine suffering is doing more literary work than a scene that is solemnly observant about the same material.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all spoken English in this novel. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for deliberate emphasis, Lena Morse's institutional register, or moments where a character is being carefully precise. If a line of dialogue uses 'do not' instead of 'don't' or 'I am' instead of 'I'm,' there should be a reason audible in the scene's pressure.
dialogue_rules.interruption_and_overlap_policy: Use interruptions, sentence fragments, and crosstalk when scene pressure warrants. Dan and Mira in particular should interrupt each other — they know each other's arguments before the sentences end. But every interruption must be motivated; do not scatter dashes for rhythmic texture alone.
dialogue_rules.leverage_shift_guidance: Every exchange of more than three lines should shift the power balance between speakers. This doesn't require overt conflict — a silence, a deflection, a piece of information withheld or accidentally revealed, a joke that lands wrong, a question left unanswered can all shift leverage. The dialogue must be going somewhere, not circling.
dialogue_rules.silence_as_dialogue: Silence, non-answers, and topic changes are valid dialogue moves and often more revealing than speech. When a character doesn't respond to something, the reader should feel the weight of what wasn't said. Render these moments through physical action or observed stillness, not by narrating that the character chose not to speak.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (single-scene compression vs. multi-scene chapters), dialogue density (some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are nearly silent), interiority depth (some chapters live mostly in the POV character's head, others are externally rendered), pacing (some chapters cover hours, others cover weeks in montage), and tonal register (comic chapters adjacent to devastating ones). The contrast between chapters is itself a narrative tool — use it to create whiplash, irony, and accumulating emotional force.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, precise, and register-sensitive. The prose absorbs the vocabulary of its setting: corporate language, tech terminology, bureaucratic formulas — but always filtered through the POV character's relationship to that language. Dan uses corporate words reflexively and with slight discomfort. Mira uses strategic vocabulary as native speech. The narrator should not editorialize on the language; let the words sit in context and let the reader hear the dissonance. Avoid: prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding, and journalistic explainer-prose that breaks character to tell the reader about the world.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating between Dan and Mira as primary POV characters. The camera stays tight to the current POV character's perceptions and vocabulary — what Dan notices is different from what Mira notices. In shared-POV chapters, the rotation should be clear (section breaks or sustained passages in one POV before shifting) rather than head-hopping within a scene.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Short declarative sentences for comic timing, for shock, and for moments of clarity. Longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interiority, sensory immersion, and emotional complexity. The rhythm should accelerate during confrontation and slow during reflection. Avoid settling into any single rhythmic default — if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong.
